body:
I'm trying to play with the toast using just this page as a reference and I feel having a section about swiping and animating the toast position could be useful.
I found out that not having `user-select: none` on the toast can make the swipe interaction weirdly sticky - it's also something to underline in the docs (or fix if possible)
It feels a bit off to call `useToast` and receive functions that operate on the whole collection of toasts. IMO `useToasts` would work better here (or `useToaster`, but that's more contentious)
Not with multiple independent versions of the library being used (e.g. extensions)
How about `useToastManager`? This matches the Global API. Maybe `Toast.Manager` would be better than `Provider`

I think `user-select: none` added to the examples is fine - it should be obvious that you don't want text selection while swiping. I added docs about stacking & animations though as that gets complex.

diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,313 @@
+# Toast
+
+<Subtitle>Generates toast notifications.</Subtitle>
+
+<Meta
+  name="description"
+  content="A high-quality, unstyled React toast component to generate notifications."
+/>
+
+<Demo path="./demos/hero" />
+
+## Usage
+
+- `<Toast.Provider>` can be wrapped around your entire app, ensuring all toasts are rendered in the same viewport.
+- <kbd>F6</kbd> lets users jump into the toast viewport landmark region to navigate toasts with
+  keyboard focus.
+
+## API reference
+
+Import the component and assemble its parts:
+
+```jsx title="Anatomy"
+import { Toast } from '@base-ui-components/react/toast';
+
+<Toast.Provider>
+  <Toast.Viewport>
+    <Toast.Root>
+      <Toast.Title />
+      <Toast.Description />
+      <Toast.Action />
+      <Toast.Close />
+    </Toast.Root>
+  </Toast.Viewport>
+</Toast.Provider>;
+```
+
+<Reference component="Toast" parts="Provider, Viewport, Root, Title, Description, Action, Close" />
+
+## useToastManager
+
+Manages toasts, called inside of a `Toast.Provider`.
+
+```tsx title="Usage"
+const toastManager = Toast.useToastManager();
+```
+
+### Return value
+
+<PropsReferenceTable
+  type="return"
+  data={{
+    toasts: {
+      type: 'Toast.Root.ToastObject[]',
+      description: 'The array of toast objects.',
+    },
+    add: {
+      type: '(options: useToast.AddOptions) => string',
+      description: 'Add a toast to the toast list.',
+    },
+    close: {
+      type: '(toastId: string) => void',
+      description: 'Closes and removes a toast from the toast list.',
+    },
+    update: {
+      type: '(toastId: string, options: useToast.UpdateOptions) => void',
+      description: 'Update a toast in the toast list.',
+    },
+    promise: {
+      type: '<Value>(promise: Promise<Value>, options: useToast.PromiseOptions) => Promise<Value>',
+      description:
+        'Create a toast that resolves with a value, with three possible states for the toast: `loading`, `success`, and `error`.',
+    },
+  }}
+/>
+
+### Method options
+
+<PropsReferenceTable
+  data={{
+    title: {
+      type: 'string',
+      description: 'The title of the toast.',
+    },
+    description: {
+      type: 'string',
+      description: 'The description of the toast.',
+    },
+    type: {
+      type: 'string',
+      description:
+        'The type of the toast. Used to conditionally style the toast or render different elements.',
+    },
+    timeout: {
+      type: 'number',
+      description: 'The amount of time (in ms) before the toast is auto dismissed.',
+    },
+    priority: {
+      type: "'low' | 'high'",
+      description: `
+        The priority of the toast.
+        - \`low\` - The toast will be announced politely.
+        - \`high\` - The toast will be announced urgently.
+      `,
+      default: "'low'",
+    },
+    onClose: {
+      type: '() => void',
+      description: 'A callback invoked when the toast is closed.',
+    },
+    onRemove: {
+      type: '() => void',
+      description:
+        'A callback invoked when the toast is removed from the list after animations complete when closed.',
+    },
+    actionProps: {
+      type: "React.ComponentPropsWithRef<'button'>",
+      description: 'The props of the action button.',
+    },
+    data: {
+      type: 'Record<string, unknown>',
+      description: 'The data of the toast.',
+    },
+  }}
+/>
+
+### `add` method
+
+Creates a toast by adding it to the toast list.
+
+Returns a `toastId` that can be used to update or close the toast later.
+
+```jsx title="Usage"
+const toastId = toastManager.add({
+  description: 'Hello, world!',
+});
+```
+
+```jsx title="Example" {2,7-9}
+function App() {
+  const toastManager = Toast.useToastManager();
+  return (
+    <button
+      type="button"
+      onClick={() => {
+        toastManager.add({
+          description: 'Hello, world!',
+        });
+      }}
+    >
+      Add toast
+    </button>
+  );
+}
+```
+
+The `title` and `description` strings are what are used to announce the toast to screen readers. Screen readers do not announce any extra content rendered inside `Toast.Root`, including the `Toast.Title` or `Toast.Description` components.
body: It would be convenient if these strings could be used as the default `children` of the Title/Description components so they can be used like `<Toast.Title />` (like our `*.Value`) components, though I'm not sure if it's very unusual for components called Title/Description to not require children 🤔

+        The priority of the toast.
+        - \`low\` - The toast will be announced politely.
+        - \`high\` - The toast will be announced urgently.
+      `,
+      default: "'low'",
+    },
+    onClose: {
+      type: '() => void',

+
+### `add` method
+
+Creates a toast by adding it to the toast list.
+
+Returns a `toastId` that can be used to update or close the toast later.
+
+```jsx title="Usage"

+});
+```
+
+```jsx title="Example" {2,7-9}
+function App() {
+  const toastManager = Toast.useToastManager();
+  return (
+    <button
+      type="button"
+      onClick={() => {
+        toastManager.add({
+          description: 'Hello, world!',
+        });
+      }}
+    >
+      Add toast
+    </button>
+  );
+}
+```
+
+The `title` and `description` strings are what are used to announce the toast to screen readers. Screen readers do not announce any extra content rendered inside `Toast.Root`, including the `Toast.Title` or `Toast.Description` components.
+
+### `update` method
+
+Updates the toast with new options.
+
+```jsx title="Usage"
+toastManager.update(toastId, {
+  description: 'New description',
+});
+```
+
+### `close` method
+
+Closes the toast, removing it from the toast list after any animations complete.
+
+```jsx title="Usage"
+toastManager.close(toastId);
+```
+
+### `promise` method
+
+Creates an asynchronous toast with three possible states: `loading`, `success`, and `error`.
+
+```tsx title="Description configuration"
+const toastId = toastManager.promise(
+  new Promise((resolve) => {
+    setTimeout(() => resolve('world!'), 1000);
+  }),
+  {
+    // Each are a shortcut for the children of `Toast.Description`
body: Isn't this the strings and not the children?

body: Since `<Field.Error>` and `<Select.Value>` both do this I think it's consistent actually

```suggestion
   * Callback function to be called when the toast is closed.
```
`'limited'` also looks like something that shouldn't be passed by the caller

It's not just creating

```suggestion
 * Returns the array of toasts and methods to manage them.
```
